notes: ## What's Changed * feat: bump md-pdf to 3.18.0 + watermark scope UI by @krata in https://github.com/Moravio/md-pdf-desktop/pull/38 * feat: include directive UX + sibling-resource render fix by @krata in https://github.com/Moravio/md-pdf-desktop/pull/39 * test: e2e regression for {% include %} resolution by @krata in https://github.com/Moravio/md-pdf-desktop/pull/40 * feat: VS-Code-style tabs + headless e2e by @krata in https://github.com/Moravio/md-pdf-desktop/pull/41 * feat: preview pane resolves {% include %} directives by @krata in https://github.com/Moravio/md-pdf-desktop/pull/42 * fix: collapsable include preview + raw assets + autolink lint by @krata in https://github.com/Moravio/md-pdf-desktop/pull/43 * feat: high-fidelity preview via @moravio/md-pdf renderToHtml by @krata in https://github.com/Moravio/md-pdf-desktop/pull/46 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Moravio/md-pdf-desktop/compare/v0.4.0...v0.5.0 --- ## Installation ### macOS 1. Download the `.dmg` and drag **md-pdf** into `/Applications`. 2. The first time you open it, macOS will refuse with *"md-pdf is damaged…"* — this app is ad-hoc signed only. Remove the quarantine flag once: ```bash xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/md-pdf.app ``` 3. Open it normally afterwards. Updates downloaded inside the app don't need this step. ### Windows Download the `.exe`, run it. SmartScreen may warn — choose *More info → Run anyway*.
